methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ERC20 nativeToken balances held by each GenesisPool (proposed/allocated/refundable amounts)
ERC20 fundingToken balances held by each GenesisPool (user deposits before launch; refundable on NOT_QUALIFIED)
ERC20 incentive tokens held by each GenesisPool (incentiveTokens[] and incentives[token])
LP token liquidity minted via IRouter.addLiquidity and held/staked via Gauge (liquidity variable and IGauge.depositsForGenesis)
ETH/BNB held by Thenian (mint proceeds) and withdrawable to multiSig via owner
ERC20 supplies of CustomToken and TokenFour (mint/burn admin-controlled inflation/deflation)
AuctionFactory-managed list of auction contracts (indirectly controls pricing/math for allocations via IAuction.getNativeTokenAmount/getFundingTokenAmount)
state_assets: GenesisPool.genesisInfo (tokenOwner, nativeToken, fundingToken, stable, duration, threshold, supplyPercent, startPrice, startTime, maturityTime)
GenesisPool.userDeposits mapping and depositers array; totalDeposits aggregate
GenesisPool.liquidity and tokenOwnerUnstaked accounting; balanceOf() share calculations
GenesisPoolManager configuration (epochController, permissionRegistory, router, pairFactory, gaugeManager, tokenHandler, genesisFactory, auctionFactory)
GenesisPoolManager thresholds (MIN_DURATION, MIN_THRESHOLD, MATURITY_TIME) and timing variables (WEEK, pre_epoch_period)
GenesisPoolManager whitelisting mapping whiteListedTokensToUser[proposedToken][tokenOwner] and token registries (nativeTokens, liveNativeTokens, indices)
GenesisPoolFactory genesisPools mapping(nativeToken => address[]) and allGenesisPools list; genesisManager address; tokenHandler address
FixedAuction has no persistent pricing state; derives allocation math from calling genesis pool
GenesisPoolAPI owner, genesisManager, genesisPoolFactory (read-only aggregation surface)
Thenian sale config (MAX_SUPPLY, NFT_PRICE, timing windows, merkle root, reserve limits) and per-user mint tracking mappings
Governor/L2Governor proposal state mappings (_proposals, _proposalVotes) and governance call queue (_governanceCall)
privilege_assets: GenesisPoolManager: GOVERNANCE role (via PermissionsRegistry.hasRole("GOVERNANCE", caller)) controlling whitelist/approve/reject/config setters and epoch controller setting
GenesisPoolManager: owner() (OwnableUpgradeable) controlling setRouter() (but implemented with a defective require)
GenesisPool: genesisManager (immutable) as primary admin for pool lifecycle state (setGenesisPoolInfo/approve/reject/transferIncentives/launch/status setters)
GenesisPool: tokenOwner authority to addIncentives and (with manager) setAuction
GenesisPool: onlyGauge authority to mutate userDeposits/tokenOwnerUnstaked via deductAmount/deductAllAmount
GenesisPoolFactory: genesisManager (onlyManager) controlling create/remove pools and ability to setGenesisManager
FixedAuction: OwnableUpgradeable present but no privileged functions besides initialize(); pricing trust is implicit via AuctionFactory selection
Thenian: owner controls withdraw(), reserveNFTs(), setRoot(), setNftPrice(), setBaseURI() (centralized sale control)
CustomToken/TokenFour: owner can mint/burn arbitrary amounts (full supply control)
Governor onlyGovernance privilege gating updateQuorumNumerator() and relay() (ability to execute arbitrary calls via proposals if integrated)
reputation_assets: Genesis pool lifecycle integrity (PRE_LISTING/PRE_LAUNCH/LAUNCH/NOT_QUALIFIED) impacting token launch credibility
Gauge/bribe distribution correctness (incentives notifications to external bribe; gauge creation) impacting LP/voter incentives trust
Governor proposal execution and quorum configuration (quorum numerator) impacting governance legitimacy
Thenian NFT mint fairness (merkle whitelist and per-wallet limits) impacting community trust

contracts_analyzed: interfaces/*.sol (IGenesisPool*, IAuction*, IGauge*, IBribe*, IRouter, ITokenHandler, IPermissionsRegistry, IERC20, etc.)
system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: GenesisPool: no inheritance (imports SafeERC20). GenesisPoolManager: OwnableUpgradeable + ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable. GenesisPoolFactory: OwnableUpgradeable. FixedAuction: OwnableUpgradeable. AuctionFactory: OwnableUpgradeable. GenesisPoolAPI: Initializable. Thenian: ERC721Enumerable + Ownable. TokenFour/CustomToken: ERC20 + Ownable. Governor/L2Governor modules: Context + ERC165 + EIP712 + IGovernor + IERC721Receiver + IERC1155Receiver, with counting/votes/quorum extensions.
external_dependencies: External protocol interfaces: IBaseV1Factory (pair factory), IRouter (AMM router), IGaugeManager/IGauge (staking/emissions), IBribe (bribes), ITokenHandler (token whitelist/connectors), IPermissionsRegistry (roles), IAuction/IAuctionFactory (auction math)
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: Multiple contracts are upgradeable implementations using OpenZeppelin Initializable/OwnableUpgradeable/ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable (GenesisPoolManager, GenesisPoolFactory, FixedAuction, AuctionFactory, GenesisPoolAPI). No proxy contract is provided in this code excerpt; deployment is assumed behind a proxy (likely Transparent/UUPS) but cannot be confirmed from provided files. GenesisPool itself is non-upgradeable (constructor + immutables).
system_architecture.access_control_structure: Role-based governance in GenesisPoolManager via external PermissionsRegistry.hasRole("GOVERNANCE", caller). Owner-based access via OwnableUpgradeable/Ownable on several contracts (GenesisPoolManager owner, AuctionFactory owner, Thenian owner, ERC20 token owners). GenesisPool uses immutable genesisManager plus tokenOwner checks and onlyGauge gating for accounting callbacks. Governor uses onlyGovernance modifier to restrict sensitive functions to execution context.
integration_points_with_other_protocols: Pair factory (IBaseV1Factory): createPair/getPair, setGenesisStatus (controls launch lifecycle on factory side)
Router (IRouter): addLiquidity used during launch; approvals set on pool tokens
GaugeManager (IGaugeManager): createGauge used during pre-launch to create gauge and bribes
Gauge (IGauge): depositsForGenesis and setGenesisPool; deduct callbacks into GenesisPool
TokenHandler (ITokenHandler): connector validation for funding token and whitelisting native token on pre-launch
Auction (IAuction): conversion functions used for allocation math and purchased() callback

state_changes: allocation approvals (ERC20 allowance state on tokens)
incentives[token] (set to 0 for each incentive token)

external_calls: _safeMint(_to, tokenId) -> onERC721Received callback if _to is contract
_safeMint(msg.sender, tokenId) -> onERC721Received callback if msg.sender is contract
_safeMint(msg.sender, tokenId) -> onERC721Received callback if msg.sender is contract
_safeMint(msg.sender, tokenId) -> onERC721Received callback if msg.sender is contract

upgradeability_surface.notes: Non-upgradeable; uses constructor and immutables (genesisManager, tokenHandler).
Upgradeable implementation (OwnableUpgradeable, ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable) but proxy pattern not included in provided code. Ensure deployed behind proxy with initialization locked on implementation.

Upgradeable implementation; critical admin is genesisManager (not owner) after initialize sets genesisManager=msg.sender. Initialization front-running risk if proxy not properly initialized atomically.
Upgradeable implementation; callable initialize sets owner. If deployed behind proxy, ensure initializer cannot be called by attacker.
Upgradeable implementation; initialize sets default auction. Ensure initialization access is controlled at deployment.
Upgradeable implementation but has its own 'owner' variable separate from OZ Ownable; no access-controlled setters exist, but initializer hijack can set references to malicious manager/factory.
